strInstructions: Soak black beans overnight, then drain. In a large pot, cook bacon and smoked sausage until browned. Add onion and garlic and sauté until fragrant. Add the beans, pork shoulder, ribs, bay leaves, and enough water to cover. Simmer slowly until the beans are tender and the meats are soft. Remove excess fat if needed, season with salt and pepper, and serve with rice, collard greens, orange slices, and farofa.

Preheat the oven and grease or line a baking sheet. Heat milk, oil, and salt until hot, then pour over tapioca flour and mix until combined. Let the mixture cool slightly. Add eggs one at a time, mixing well, then add grated cheese and knead into a sticky dough. Shape into small balls and place on the baking sheet. Bake until puffed, lightly golden, and chewy inside.

Season fish and shrimp with lime juice, garlic, salt, and pepper. In a wide pot, layer onions, bell peppers, tomatoes, and the seafood. Add coconut milk and dendê oil, then simmer gently until the fish is cooked and the vegetables are soft. Avoid stirring too much so the fish stays intact. Finish with cilantro and serve with rice, farofa, or pirão.

Combine sweetened condensed milk, cocoa powder, butter, and a pinch of salt in a saucepan. Cook over medium-low heat, stirring constantly, until the mixture thickens and pulls away from the bottom of the pan. Transfer to a greased plate and let cool. Grease your hands, roll the mixture into small balls, and coat with chocolate sprinkles.

Cook chicken until tender, then shred it. Sauté onion and garlic, then add shredded chicken, tomato paste, parsley, and seasoning. Make the dough by bringing chicken broth and butter to a boil, then stirring in flour until a smooth dough forms. Let cool slightly. Flatten small portions of dough, fill with chicken, and shape into teardrops. Dip in egg and breadcrumbs, then fry until golden and crisp.

Soak black-eyed peas, then remove the skins as much as possible. Blend the peas with onion and salt into a thick paste. Beat or stir the paste until airy. Shape into small fritters and deep-fry in hot dendê oil until crisp and golden. Split each acarajé and fill with vatapá, dried shrimp, salad, and hot pepper sauce.

Soak bread in coconut milk until soft. Blend the soaked bread with peanuts, cashews, dried shrimp, onion, garlic, ginger, and a little coconut milk until smooth. Transfer to a pot and cook over medium heat, stirring constantly, until thick. Add dendê oil and more coconut milk as needed. Cook until creamy and fragrant, then serve with rice, acarajé, or seafood.

Melt butter in a skillet over medium heat. Add onion and cook until softened. Add garlic and cook briefly until fragrant. Stir in cassava flour and toast, stirring often, until golden and nutty. Season with salt and pepper. Add parsley if desired and serve as a side for feijoada, grilled meats, or rice and beans.

Rinse rice if desired and drain well. Heat oil in a saucepan and sauté onion and garlic until fragrant. Add the rice and stir for a few minutes until the grains are coated and lightly toasted. Add hot water and salt. Bring to a boil, reduce heat, cover, and cook until the rice is tender and the water is absorbed. Let rest before fluffing.

Soak beans if desired, then cook with water and bay leaves until tender. In a separate skillet, heat oil and sauté onion and garlic until golden. Mash a small portion of cooked beans into the sautéed aromatics to thicken, then return everything to the pot. Simmer until creamy and season with salt and pepper. Serve with Brazilian rice.

Cook cassava until very soft, then blend with coconut milk into a creamy puree. Season shrimp with lime, salt, and pepper. In a pot, sauté onion, garlic, bell pepper, and tomatoes until soft. Add the shrimp and cook briefly. Stir in the cassava puree and dendê oil, then simmer until thick and creamy. Finish with cilantro and serve with rice.

Desalt the dried beef by soaking and changing the water, then cook until tender and shred. Sauté onion and garlic in butter or oil, then add the shredded beef and season well. Cook cassava until soft, then mash with butter, milk or cream, and salt. Spread the beef in a baking dish, cover with cassava puree, and top with cheese. Bake until hot and golden.

Season beef cubes with salt and pepper. Brown the beef in oil, then remove. Sauté onion and garlic until fragrant. Return the beef to the pot with tomatoes, tomato paste, bay leaf, broth or water, and seasonings. Simmer until the beef is tender. Add potatoes and carrots near the end and cook until soft. Serve with rice and beans.

Cut beef into small cubes and season with salt and pepper. Brown the beef in oil, then add onion and garlic. Stir in tomato paste, Worcestershire sauce, broth, and seasonings. Simmer until the beef is tender and the sauce is thick. Serve with rice, beans, farofa, fried egg, or fried banana.

Season chicken pieces with garlic, lime juice, salt, and pepper. Brown the chicken in oil, then remove. Sauté onion and garlic in the same pot. Return the chicken and add tomatoes, broth, and seasonings. Simmer until the chicken is tender. In a separate pan, sauté okra until less slimy, then add it to the chicken and cook briefly. Serve with rice and angu or polenta.

Season chicken with garlic, lime juice, salt, pepper, and spices. Brown the chicken in oil, then remove. Sauté onion, garlic, bell pepper, and tomatoes. Add rice and stir to coat in the aromatics. Return the chicken to the pot, add broth or water, turmeric or saffron, and simmer covered until the rice is cooked and the chicken is tender. Finish with parsley or cilantro.

Cut chicken into bite-sized pieces and season with salt and pepper. Sauté onion and garlic in butter or oil, then add chicken and cook until browned. Stir in ketchup, mustard, tomato sauce, and mushrooms if using. Simmer until the chicken is cooked through and the sauce thickens. Turn off the heat and stir in heavy cream. Serve with rice and potato sticks.

Slice beef into strips and season with salt and pepper. Sear quickly in butter or oil until browned, then remove. Sauté onion and garlic, then add tomato paste, ketchup, mustard, and mushrooms. Return the beef and simmer briefly until tender. Turn off the heat and stir in heavy cream. Serve with white rice and crispy potato sticks.

Cook beans until tender, then blend or mash with some cooking liquid. In a skillet, cook bacon or sausage until browned. Add onion and garlic and sauté until fragrant. Stir in the mashed beans and slowly add cassava flour until thick and creamy. Season with salt and pepper. Serve with rice, collard greens, fried eggs, and pork.

Cook potatoes in broth or water until very tender, then blend or mash until smooth. In a pot, sauté sliced sausage until browned. Add onion and garlic and cook until fragrant. Pour in the potato broth and simmer. Add finely sliced collard greens and cook just until tender. Season with salt and pepper and serve hot with olive oil and bread.

Preheat the oven and grease small molds with butter and sugar. In a bowl, mix egg yolks, sugar, coconut milk, melted butter, and shredded coconut until smooth. Let the mixture rest briefly so the coconut hydrates. Pour into the prepared molds. Bake in a water bath until the custard is set and the tops are glossy and golden. Cool before unmolding.

Combine sweetened condensed milk, butter, and shredded coconut in a saucepan. Cook over medium-low heat, stirring constantly, until the mixture thickens and pulls away from the bottom of the pan. Transfer to a greased plate and let cool. Grease your hands, roll into small balls, coat in shredded coconut or sugar, and decorate each with a clove if desired.

Blend carrots, eggs, and oil until smooth. In a bowl, combine flour, sugar, and baking powder. Pour in the carrot mixture and stir until smooth. Transfer to a greased cake pan and bake until a toothpick comes out clean. For the chocolate glaze, cook cocoa powder, sugar, butter, and milk until glossy and slightly thick. Pour the warm glaze over the cake and let set before slicing.

Combine sugar and water in a saucepan and cook until syrupy. Add shredded coconut and stir constantly until the mixture thickens and begins to pull away from the pan. Add condensed milk if making a creamier version and continue cooking until firm enough to scoop. Drop spoonfuls onto a greased surface or parchment paper and let cool completely before serving.

Soak white hominy overnight, then drain. Cook the hominy with water until tender. Add milk, coconut milk, sweetened condensed milk, sugar, cinnamon, and cloves. Simmer slowly, stirring often, until thick and creamy. Remove whole spices if desired and serve warm or chilled with ground cinnamon on top.

Cut the kernels from fresh corn cobs. Blend the corn with milk until smooth, then strain through a fine sieve, pressing well to extract the liquid. Pour the corn milk into a saucepan and add sugar and a pinch of salt. Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly, until thick and creamy. Pour into dishes and sprinkle with cinnamon. Serve warm or chilled.

Slice guava paste into thick squares or rectangles. Slice queijo minas, queijo fresco, or another mild white cheese into similar-sized pieces. Arrange one slice of guava paste with one slice of cheese on each plate or skewer them together. Serve at room temperature as a simple Brazilian dessert.
strIngredient2: Queijo Minas or Fresh White Cheese

strInstructions: Prepare or use pastel dough. Sauté onion and garlic in oil until fragrant. Add ground beef and cook until browned. Season with salt, pepper, cumin, and parsley. Let the filling cool, then place spoonfuls onto pieces of dough. Fold into rectangles or half-moons and seal the edges well. Deep-fry in hot oil until golden and crisp. Serve hot.

Cut pastel dough into rectangles. Place cheese filling in the center of each piece, being careful not to overfill. Fold the dough over the cheese and seal the edges tightly with a fork. Heat oil in a deep pan and fry the pastels until puffed, crisp, and golden. Drain briefly and serve immediately while the cheese is melted.

Bring milk, butter, and salt to a boil, then add flour all at once and stir until a smooth dough forms. Let the dough cool slightly, then roll it out and cut circles. Fill each circle with chopped ham and cheese, fold into half-moons, and seal. Dip in beaten egg and coat with breadcrumbs. Fry until golden and crisp.

Prepare a buttery pie dough and chill it. Cook chicken until tender, then shred. Sauté onion and garlic, then add tomatoes, shredded chicken, corn, olives, peas, and seasonings. Stir in cream cheese or requeijão to make the filling creamy. Line a baking dish with dough, add the chicken filling, cover with more dough, and brush with egg yolk. Bake until golden.

Cut chicken into small pieces and season with garlic, lime juice, salt, pepper, and herbs. Let it marinate for at least 30 minutes. Heat oil in a deep pan and fry the chicken pieces until deeply golden, crisp, and cooked through. Drain on paper towels. Fry sliced garlic briefly until golden and scatter over the chicken. Serve with lime wedges.
strMeasure2: 6 cloves, minced plus sliced for topping

strInstructions: Season ground beef and shape into burger patties. Cook the patties on a hot skillet or grill until browned and cooked to preference. Fry bacon and eggs separately. Toast the buns, then assemble with mayonnaise, lettuce, tomato, beef patty, ham, cheese, bacon, fried egg, corn, peas, and potato sticks. Serve immediately.

Spread butter on the outside of bread slices. Layer ham and cheese between the slices. Cook the sandwich in a skillet, sandwich press, or griddle until the bread is golden and crisp and the cheese is melted. Serve hot for breakfast or a simple snack.

Cook hot dogs in simmering water or pan-fry until heated through. Prepare a tomato sauce with onion, garlic, tomatoes, tomato sauce, corn, peas, salt, and pepper. Place each hot dog in a bun and spoon sauce over it. Top with mashed potatoes, potato sticks, mayonnaise, ketchup, mustard, and grated cheese if desired.

Slice the French roll and remove some of the soft inside if desired. Layer roast beef inside the bread. Melt mozzarella in hot water or under a broiler until soft, then place over the beef. Add tomato slices and pickles. Close the sandwich and serve warm.

Desalt salt cod by soaking and changing the water. Cook the cod until tender, then flake it. Boil potatoes until soft and mash them. Mix cod, mashed potatoes, onion, garlic, parsley, egg, salt, and pepper into a thick mixture. Shape into small croquettes or balls. Fry in hot oil until golden and crisp. Serve with lime wedges.

Sauté onion, garlic, bell pepper, and tomatoes in oil until soft. Add crab meat, lime juice, coconut milk if using, parsley or cilantro, salt, and pepper. Cook until the mixture is moist but not watery. Spoon into crab shells or ramekins, sprinkle with breadcrumbs or Parmesan, and bake until golden on top. Serve with lime wedges.

Season fish with lime juice, garlic, salt, and pepper. In a clay pot or wide pan, layer onion, tomatoes, cilantro, and the fish. Drizzle with olive oil and annatto oil if using. Add a little water or fish stock. Simmer gently until the fish is cooked through and the vegetables create a light broth. Serve with rice and pirão.

Bring fish broth or moqueca broth to a simmer. Slowly sprinkle in cassava flour while stirring constantly to avoid lumps. Continue stirring until the mixture thickens into a smooth porridge-like texture. Adjust salt and pepper. Serve hot as a side dish with moqueca or fish stew.

Cook the beans with bay leaves until tender but still whole, then drain and set aside. In a large skillet, cook bacon until crisp, then add sliced sausage and brown it well. Add onion and garlic and sauté until fragrant. Stir in collard greens and cook until wilted. Add the cooked beans and scrambled or chopped eggs, then gradually stir in cassava flour until the mixture is moist but crumbly. Season with salt and pepper and finish with parsley.
searchAliases: brazilian beans with sausage and collard greens

strInstructions: Heat oil in a large pot and brown the sausage if using. Add onion and cook until translucent, then add garlic and cook briefly. Stir in shredded carne seca or leftover barbecue beef and cook until lightly browned. Add rice and stir until coated with the fat and aromatics. Pour in hot water, season with salt and pepper, and simmer covered until the rice is tender and the liquid is absorbed. Finish with parsley and serve hot.
strIngredient1: Cooked Churrasco Beef or Carne Seca

strInstructions: Sauté onion, garlic, bell pepper, tomatoes, and tomato sauce in oil until softened. Add corn, peas, olives, sardines or tuna, shrimp if using, parsley, and seasonings. Pour in broth and bring to a simmer. Gradually stir in cornmeal or flaked corn flour until the mixture thickens and pulls together. Decorate a ring mold with sliced eggs, tomatoes, sardines, and olives, then spoon in the cuscuz mixture and press gently. Let set before unmolding.
strMeasure6: 3 pieces, chopped plus slices for garnish

strInstructions: Preheat the oven and grease a cake pan. Blend corn kernels with milk until smooth. Add coconut milk, cornmeal, sugar, oil, eggs, shredded coconut, and salt, then blend or pulse until combined. Add baking powder and pulse briefly just to incorporate. Pour the batter into the prepared pan and bake until golden and a toothpick comes out clean. Cool before slicing.

Remove the husks from fresh corn carefully and reserve the larger husks for wrapping. Cut the kernels from the cobs and blend or process with milk, sugar, butter, and salt until creamy. Spoon the corn mixture into softened corn husks, fold into packets, and tie securely. Boil or steam the packets until firm and cooked through. Serve warm as a sweet or savory corn dish.

Peel the cassava and remove the tough center core. Cut into thick sticks and boil in salted water until tender but not falling apart. Drain and let dry completely. Heat oil in a deep pan and fry the cassava pieces until golden and crisp on the outside. Drain, season with salt, and serve hot with lime wedges or dipping sauce.

Add coconut milk, sweetened condensed milk, cachaça, and ice to a blender. Blend until smooth, creamy, and chilled. Taste and adjust sweetness or strength as desired. Pour into small glasses and serve immediately.

Cut the lime into wedges and place in a glass with sugar. Muddle gently to release the lime juice and oils without crushing the peel too much. Fill the glass with ice and add cachaça. Stir well until the sugar begins to dissolve and the drink is chilled. Serve immediately.

Blend frozen açaí puree with banana and a small amount of liquid until thick and smooth. Pour into a bowl. Top with granola, sliced banana, berries, coconut, honey, or other toppings. Serve immediately while cold and thick.

Prepare a chocolate cream by cooking milk, condensed milk, cocoa powder or chocolate, cornstarch, and egg yolks until thick. Let it cool slightly. Dip biscuits briefly in milk and layer them in a dish. Spread chocolate cream over the biscuits, then repeat layers until the dish is filled. Top with whipped cream, chocolate shavings, or cocoa powder. Chill for several hours before serving.
